title: Consequences for Social Security Fraud Act
short_title: Consequences for Social Security Fraud Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
crs_summary: Consequences for Social Security Fraud Act This bill adds additional criminal offences to the grounds upon which a non-U.S. national ( alien under federal law) may be barred from admission into the United States or deported. Specifically, an individual who has been convicted of, or admits to committing, Social Security fraud, identification document fraud, or fraud related to COVID-19 financial assistance programs is inadmissible or deportable under the bill.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
